pages.title: About Me | Lewis Cocking
pages.meta_description: I live in Broxbourne and I'm proud to call it home. I was educated here, at Wormley Primary School and John Warner Secondary School, and my first job was in Hoddesdon town centre.
headings: h2: One year as your MP
pages.text_excerpt: About Me | Lewis Cocking Skip to main content Lewis Cocking MP Main navigation Home About Me News My Priorities In Parliament Contact Surgeries Lewis Cocking MP About Me Tweet I live in Broxbourne and I'm proud to call it home. I was educated here, at Wormley Primary School and John Warner Secondary School, and my first job was in Hoddesdon town centre. I grew up with Conservative values. My mum worked in a factory and my dad was a mechanic. They instilled in me that it was only through hard work that you could provide more opportunities and an improved quality of life for your family. My grandfather ran his own businesses, demonstrating to me the vital role small businesses play in our community. Having lived here my whole life, I was honoured to be elected a local councillor in 2016, then Leader of Broxbourne Council three years later. Prior to becoming leader, Broxbourne was the only district in Hertfordshire to have negative economic growth. In my time in charge, this was completely transformed. In my role, I emphasised the importance of job creation and encouraged business investment by cutting bureaucracy. Broxbourne is now open for business, and I left the Council as one of the most business-friendly in the UK. Together, we have attracted billions of pounds of private sector investment, including £400M for a new town centre. This will bring thousands of jobs to the area, and I'm determined to help local people develop the skills required to access them. From 2021 to 2024, I was the Deputy Police and Crime Commissioner for Hertfordshire. This role provided a unique insight into the work of our brave police force, and first-hand experience of the red tape holding back our public services. I have a strong track record of delivery and have gained a reputation as someone who gets things done in all the roles I’ve held. I am not afraid of working hard and, as your MP, will work tirelessly to deliver prosperity and security for everyone in Broxbourne. News One year as your MP Friday, 4 July, 2025 Looking back on my first 12 months. pages.text_excerpt: In Parliament | Lewis Cocking Skip to main content Lewis Cocking MP Main navigation Home About Me News My Priorities In Parliament Contact Surgeries Lewis Cocking MP In Parliament Tweet How I'm representing you in Parliament. News Will changes to SEND deliver fairer funding for Broxbourne? Wednesday, 25 February, 2026 The Department for Education have published their plans to change the special educational needs system. Youth Unemployment Monday, 2 February, 2026 The number of young people who are not in education, employment or training is at its highest level for more than a decade. Keir Starmer's message to Broxbourne Tuesday, 9 December, 2025 Challenging the Prime Minister on his decision to punish those in work. Will the Prime Minister tackle roadworks? Wednesday, 26 November, 2025 My Question to Keir Starmer. Demanding action on flooding Wednesday, 17 September, 2025 Flooding is a huge concern for people in Stanstead Abbotts and across Broxbourne. We must keep the Chagos Islands Tuesday, 16 September, 2025 My speech on the second reading of the Bill that would see the Chagos Islands, which is the site of an important UK military base, be transferred to Mauritius. Celebrating Hearts for Herts Charity in Parliament Tuesday, 2 September, 2025 Paying tribute to a Hoddesdon-based charity in Parliament. When will the Government close the Cheshunt asylum hotel? Monday, 1 September, 2025 Challenging the Home Secretary. Demanding the closure of the asylum hotel in Cheshunt Tuesday, 22 July, 2025 I have always opposed the hotel in Cheshunt being used to house illegal asylum seekers. One year as your MP Friday, 4 July, 2025 Looking back on my first 12 months. Load More In Parliament Westminster Trips What can an MP do?
